multipleTestingNote: 5 pre-registered event(s) x 7 horizons = 35 cells screened. P-values are NOT corrected for multiple testing; treat a single green cell among many as weak evidence.

events.name: SPY RSI: overbought to sub-50 in 3 days
events.rationale: A fast collapse from overbought (RSI > 70) to sub-50 within three sessions marks an abrupt loss of momentum without a full trend break. The published SentimentTrader/DailyChartbook version reads it as a short-term washout that historically preceded a bounce. We replicate it on SPY to validate the engine against a known study, knowing our 10-year daily window gives a thin sample.
events.definition: Trigger on the session RSI(14) crosses below 50, provided RSI exceeded 70 within the prior 3 sessions.
events.entryNote: Forward return measured from the close of the trigger day (event-study convention).

events.name: Cross-asset breadth crosses below 50% above 200d SMA
events.rationale: Cross-sectional participation across the whole 56-ETF cross-asset book, not an index level. When fewer than half of all assets — equities, bonds, commodities, real estate, currencies, alternatives — are above their own 200-day SMA, trends are failing broadly and there are few places to hide, even if cap-weighted SPY has not broken. This is a breadth construct a single-index desk cannot build, and it maps onto the same 200d-SMA regime gate the backtest already trusts. Forward returns are measured on SPY.
events.definition: Trigger on the session the share of the 56-ETF cross-asset universe trading above its own 200-day SMA crosses below 50% (minimum 30 eligible names).
events.entryNote: Forward return measured from the close of the trigger day (event-study convention).

events.name: EWJ at sustained 1-year highs into thinning cross-asset breadth
events.rationale: A single-country equity index pressing repeated one-year highs while cross-asset participation has rolled over is a late-cycle narrowing: the advance is carried by fewer independent engines across the whole 56-ETF opportunity set, so an adverse shock has fewer offsets and trend-following capital is concentrated. We measure the condition on EWJ, our liquid Japan proxy (MSCI Japan; roughly 0.9 correlated to the Nikkei 225, adequate for the price-new-high gate). This deliberately substitutes our cross-asset breadth for the single-market internal breadth used by the external study that prompted it; it therefore tests participation-narrowing across asset classes, not Japanese index internals, and imports none of that study's printed statistics. Significance is judged solely by our random-entry Monte Carlo. The sample is necessarily thin on a ten-year daily window, so treat a single favourable horizon as weak evidence. If a position results, it is expressed via DXJ (JPY-hedged); forward returns here are measured on EWJ.
events.definition: Trigger on the onset (condition true today, not true yesterday) of: EWJ closed at a 1-year high on at least 9 of the trailing 30 sessions AND cross-asset breadth (share of the 56-ETF universe above its own 200-day SMA, minimum 30 eligible names) sits at least 15 percentage points below its own trailing-1-year peak. Forward returns measured on EWJ from the trigger close.
events.entryNote: Forward return measured from the close of the trigger day (event-study convention).

events.name: GLD oversold washout below its 200-day SMA
events.rationale: A mean-reversion proxy, NOT a replication of the SentimenTrader GLD study (which rests on a proprietary Optix sentiment index and a 21-year seasonal window we cannot reproduce on our ten-year daily history). Mechanism: a liquid, widely-held commodity ETF that becomes acutely oversold (RSI below 30) while already trading below its 200-day SMA has usually overshot to the downside on momentum or forced selling, so a partial snap-back toward the mean is common over the following weeks. This is deliberately a COUNTER-TREND test, outside this scanner's trend-following spine and its 200-day regime gate; it is logged and reported as such, not as a trend signal. Forward returns on GLD; significance from our random-entry Monte Carlo. The sample is thin on a ten-year daily window, so treat a single favourable horizon as weak evidence.
events.definition: Trigger on the session GLD's RSI(14) crosses below 30 while GLD closes below its 200-day SMA. Forward returns measured on GLD from the trigger close.
events.entryNote: Forward return measured from the close of the trigger day (event-study convention).

events.name: SLV oversold washout below its 200-day SMA
events.rationale: The same oversold-in-downtrend mean-reversion construct as the GLD card, applied to silver to test whether the washout-bounce holds across precious metals. Silver (SLV) is higher-beta and more volatile than gold, so washouts run deeper and snap-backs are larger but noisier; this is the natural robustness check on the gold result. Counter-trend by design, outside the trend-following spine. Forward returns on SLV; significance from our random-entry Monte Carlo; thin sample caveat applies.
events.definition: Trigger on the session SLV's RSI(14) crosses below 30 while SLV closes below its 200-day SMA. Forward returns measured on SLV from the trigger close.
events.entryNote: Forward return measured from the close of the trigger day (event-study convention).
